  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To make this stronger, should we prioritize (A) language-level tightening without changing claims (writing polish), (B) strengthening the research argument—sharper thesis/gap, novelty positioning, claim–evidence alignment, and section flow, or (C) both with equal emphasis?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely requires sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing to emphasize the problem and solution at the abstract level.",
    "R1.2": "Making it stronger entails checking the internal logic from ambiguity detection through abstention to the stated performance gains.",
    "R1.3": "Persuasiveness depends on ensuring each claim is directly supported by the reported metrics and evaluations.",
    "R1.4": "The explicit 'first work' assertion benefits from assessing delta versus nearest prior approaches to avoid weak novelty framing.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering or emphasizing sections can improve the narrative arc and highlight contributions and evidence more forcefully.",
    "R1.6": "Tighter, more assertive prose can materially increase perceived strength without altering technical content.",
    "RX.1": "Disclosing uncertainties and limitations maintains credibility while strengthening claims.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding unqualified superlatives or causal overreach ensures stronger yet defensible positioning.",
    "RX.3": "The effort should stay within strengthening the given text rather than expanding scope beyond the artifact.",
    "RX.4": "Careful phrasing reduces risk of overstating capabilities in safety-critical manipulation contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should preserve traceability of datasets, training details, and released artifacts referenced in the text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To make this stronger, should we prioritize (A) language-level tightening without changing claims (writing polish), (B) strengthening the research argument—sharper thesis/gap, novelty positioning, claim–evidence alignment, and section flow, or (C) both with equal emphasis?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely requires sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing to emphasize the problem and solution at the abstract level.
rationale.R1.2: Making it stronger entails checking the internal logic from ambiguity detection through abstention to the stated performance gains.
rationale.R1.3: Persuasiveness depends on ensuring each claim is directly supported by the reported metrics and evaluations.
rationale.R1.4: The explicit 'first work' assertion benefits from assessing delta versus nearest prior approaches to avoid weak novelty framing.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering or emphasizing sections can improve the narrative arc and highlight contributions and evidence more forcefully.
rationale.R1.6: Tighter, more assertive prose can materially increase perceived strength without altering technical content.
rationale.RX.1: Disclosing uncertainties and limitations maintains credibility while strengthening claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding unqualified superlatives or causal overreach ensures stronger yet defensible positioning.
rationale.RX.3: The effort should stay within strengthening the given text rather than expanding scope beyond the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Careful phrasing reduces risk of overstating capabilities in safety-critical manipulation contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should preserve traceability of datasets, training details, and released artifacts referenced in the text.
